intro.headline: SUIT in the Community
intro.subtext: Art, music, drama, events, and five years of making a difference in Wolverhampton. This is our living story — from the streets to the stage to the British Library.

posts.title: SUIT Book Chapter Placed in the British Library National Archives
posts.body: In January 2026, staff from SUIT attended the British Library in London to help launch a groundbreaking new book on addiction and recovery. The book, 'Lived Experience Recovery Organisations', was co-authored by internationally renowned experts Dame Carol Black and Dr Ed Day. Created by Dr David Patton and Dot Smith of Recovery Connections, the book has been deposited in the British Library, ensuring that SUIT's work is recognised as a meaningful contribution to national knowledge of lived experience recovery.
posts.quote: To have our work recognised in such a prestigious publication really does validate, not only what we do here in Wolverhampton, but what the LERO movement is achieving nationally.

posts.title: [P R O] F I L E Residency — 'Joey the Fish' at Wolverhampton Art Gallery
posts.body: SUIT/Recovery Near You client David's animation work 'Joey the Fish' went live at Wolverhampton Art Gallery on 24th January 2026. The installation is a deep dive into David's lived experience of struggles with alcohol and its consequences, and how he swam to the top again. The project, delivered by Asylum Artist Quarter and funded by Arts Council England, gives SUIT clients an incredible experience — undertaking a programme where they can access artist studios and skills workshops.
posts.quote: Joey inhabits an ocean disrupted by urbanisation and human neglect. A discarded beer can frequently appears — a small, ordinary object that carries disproportionate weight. It signals addiction, harm, and the quiet normalisation of behaviour that can have serious personal and environmental consequences.

posts.title: Mental Health Peer Support Drop-Ins — Monthly at the Station Hub
posts.body: A safe space open to all who may need support, advice, and community links to services including health & wellbeing, advocacy, housing, welfare, education & training, employment, activities & groups, addiction & recovery, mental health, and delivering peer-led initiatives. Workshops are themed with activities and delivered by staff and volunteers working with SUIT, the Good Shepherd, One Wolverhampton, Wolverhampton City Council's Department of Public Health, Rethink, and the NHS. First Friday of every month, 10am-12pm at Train Station Hub WV1 1LE.

posts.title: Community Liver Scan Clinics with Recovery Near You & Cultural Engagement Team
posts.body: Over 1,200 liver scans have been completed at a pioneering series of pop-up clinics across Wolverhampton designed to bring non-invasive health screening directly into the community. By teaming up with Recovery Near You and SUIT, and working alongside faith leaders, lived experience volunteers, businesses, and community organisations, the programme has demonstrated the value of culturally sensitive, accessible, and health-focused interventions in breaking down stigma.
posts.quote: These community Fibro-scan clinics are a fantastic example of how we are working with partners to bring healthcare into familiar, welcoming spaces.
posts.quoteAuthor: Councillor Obaida Ahmed, Cabinet Member for Health

posts.title: Yoga with Cate — Wellbeing Sessions at SUIT
posts.body: Cate brings regular yoga sessions to SUIT, supporting recovery through movement, mindfulness, and physical wellbeing. 'Yoga has been part of my life since my twenties — sometimes front and centre, sometimes in the background — but always there when I've needed it. It helps me keep choosing what truly supports me, rather than slipping back into old patterns.' SUIT wants to say thank you for her continued support and patronage.

posts.title: Halloween Tattoo Bash 2025 — SUIT as Donation Organisation
posts.body: We had an incredible time at the Halloween Tattoo Bash on 2nd November 2025 at Wolverhampton Racecourse. There were vibrant and engaging stalls, wonderful conversations, and the art & tattoos were exceptional. This year's Halloween Tattoo Bash kindly picked SUIT as their donation organisation and we are humbled by the sincere and genuine support for our lived experience service. All donations go through WVCA and are ring-fenced for SUIT's work as a LERO in Wolverhampton's recovery community.

posts.title: National Recovery Walk 2025 — West Park, Wolverhampton
posts.body: The National Recovery Walk was an exceptional celebration of our recovery community in Wolverhampton. The Favor UK event, held on September 13th in Wolverhampton's beautiful West Park, was a true reflection of the unity, diversity, creativity, and strength of our city. The partnership between SUIT, Recovery Near You, the Good Shepherd, and Wolverhampton Today Public Health has put our city at the forefront of addiction support in the UK.

posts.title: Favor UK National Recovery Conference 2025 at Molineux
posts.body: Our team had a brilliant day at Wolverhampton Wanderers FC Molineux for the Favor UK National Conference. On display was artwork by SUIT, Recovery Near You, & Good Shepherd clients. Speakers included Pat McFadden MP, Dr Ed Day, Dr Jane Booth, and community hero Alan Jarvis. There were inspirational lived experience shares from Good Shepherd, NICS, and Navraj Dhesi of No More Pretending. An enormous thank you to Neil Reading, Artistic Director of the Arena Theatre, for writing and narrating our powerful immersive drama experience.

posts.title: 'The Lack of Giving a S**t, and How to Overcome It' — Arena Theatre
posts.body: On 15th November 2024, SUIT presented a powerful piece of community theatre at the Arena Theatre in Wolverhampton. The performance explored themes of apathy, stigma, and the transformational power of human connection, performed by SUIT service users alongside professional actors. Photography by Steph Teague.

posts.title: Wolverhampton PRIDE 2024 — Celebrating Inclusivity in Recovery
posts.body: Pride is a wonderful celebration of inclusivity, identity, freedom, and LGBT+ rights. We attended with Recovery Near You, with exciting and colourful outreach stalls, raising awareness towards support and information about our essential lived experience service. SUIT believes that recovery should be accessible, inclusive, and celebrated by everyone in our community.

posts.title: DDN Conference 2024 — SUIT Featured in DDN Magazine
posts.body: SUIT were featured in the September 2024 issue of DDN Magazine and delivered a presentation at the DDN Conference at The National Motorcycle Museum, Birmingham. Speaking were Marcus Johnson, Sanjeev Kumar, Karolina Sowinska, and Christiane Jenkins.

posts.title: #poPARTofrecovery — Community Art Exhibition 2024
posts.body: The #poPARTofrecovery exhibition showcased stunning artwork from SUIT service users, using Pop Art themes to explore identity, recovery, and self-expression. Photos and film still art by Christiane. The exhibition demonstrated how creativity can be a powerful vehicle for processing trauma and building self-worth. Open to partners, families, and the wider community.

posts.title: 'The Forest Faced' — 2025 Exhibition by Christiane
posts.body: 'The Forest Faced' brought together photography and film still art exploring the intersection of nature, recovery, and hidden identity. Created by Christiane Jenkins, SUIT's Creative Arts and Research Lead, and photographed by Peter Chand, the exhibition was part of SUIT's ongoing commitment to using creative arts as a pathway to healing.

posts.title: Asylum Artist Quarter Partnership — Studio Access for Recovery
posts.body: SUIT's partnership with Asylum Artist Quarter in the city centre provides service users with access to professional artist studios, skills workshops, and the opportunity to develop their artistic practice. Funded by Arts Council England, the programme creates multiple showcasing opportunities with a focus on raising the profile and experience of individuals entering the professional creative sector and including lived experience in the cultural ecology of Wolverhampton.

posts.title: DDN Conference 2023 — 15th Annual Conference, Birmingham
posts.body: SUIT delivered a memorable presentation at the 15th DDN Conference at The National Motorcycle Museum in Birmingham. The team shared their model of lived experience peer-led support and the outcomes of their wraparound service model. Featured in DDN's September 2023 magazine issue.

posts.title: Annual Aquarius Recovery Conference 2024
posts.body: SUIT attended the Annual Aquarius Recovery Conference in 2024, connecting with recovery organisations across the region and showcasing Wolverhampton's lived experience model. These conferences bring together treatment providers, LEROs, and policymakers to share best practice.

posts.title: SUIT Joins the College of Lived Experience (CLERO)
posts.body: SUIT became a proud member of the College of Lived Experience (CLERO), connecting with other lived experience organisations nationally and committing to an important set of standards surrounding peer-led support. This membership recognises SUIT as a national LERO working to government-defined recovery support standards.

posts.title: Wolverhampton Literature Festival — Spoken Word & Storytelling
posts.body: SUIT service users performed spoken word and shared their recovery stories at the Wolverhampton Literature Festival. Using creative writing and performance as therapeutic tools, participants explored their experiences through art, building confidence and connecting with the wider cultural community. The sessions were facilitated by Christiane Jenkins and supported by partner organisations.

posts.title: Drama Workshops with Geese Theatre Company
posts.body: SUIT's ongoing collaboration with Geese Theatre Company continued with drama workshops exploring themes of identity, choice, and change. Geese Theatre uses innovative mask and theatre techniques specifically designed for criminal justice and community settings. The sessions gave SUIT service users a safe space to explore their stories through performance.

posts.title: Head4Health Programme with Wolves Foundation
posts.body: SUIT clients participated in the Wolves Foundation Head4Health programme, using the power of sport and physical activity to improve mental wellbeing. The sessions included group exercise, mindfulness, and peer support, connecting people in recovery with positive physical and social activity at Wolverhampton Wanderers' facilities.

posts.title: Cultural Engagement Team Launches — Reaching Every Community
posts.body: SUIT launched its dedicated Cultural Engagement Team to ensure that recovery support reaches every community in Wolverhampton. With Vijay Sahota providing Punjabi-speaking support and Karolina Sowinska providing Polish-speaking support, SUIT expanded its culturally sensitive outreach to break down barriers of language, stigma, and cultural understanding around addiction and recovery.

posts.title: Hospital DALT Service — Peer Support on the Wards
posts.body: SUIT launched its Hospital DALT (Drug & Alcohol Liaison Team) service at New Cross Hospital, with Matt Whittall providing peer support directly on hospital wards. This groundbreaking initiative puts lived experience support at the heart of clinical treatment, catching people at their most vulnerable and connecting them to recovery services before discharge.

posts.title: SMART Recovery Groups — Mutual Aid at SUIT
posts.body: SUIT's weekly SMART Recovery groups provide a science-based, self-empowering approach to addiction recovery. Facilitated by Warren Sutherland, sessions focus on building motivation, coping with urges, managing thoughts and feelings, and living a balanced life. Open to anyone in the Wolverhampton community.

posts.title: Weekly Art Group — Finding Expression Through Creativity
posts.body: SUIT's weekly Art Group has become a cornerstone of our creative recovery programme. Led by Christiane Jenkins, sessions explore a wide range of media and techniques, giving service users a therapeutic outlet for self-expression. The group has produced exhibition-quality work that has been displayed across Wolverhampton and at national conferences.

posts.title: Music & Wellbeing Sessions — Sound as Recovery
posts.body: SUIT introduced music and wellbeing sessions as part of our creative recovery programme. Using rhythm, songwriting, and collaborative music-making, participants explore emotional expression and build connections. Music sessions complement our art, drama, and yoga offerings, reinforcing the principle that recovery is about building a life worth living.

posts.title: Recovery Ambassador Programme — From Service User to Mentor
posts.body: SUIT's structured Recovery Ambassador Programme continues to transform lives by helping people in recovery build skills, gain qualifications, and find purpose through peer-led volunteering. Ambassadors are supported with relapse prevention plans, regular supervision, and relevant training. Opportunities include facilitating groups, providing one-to-one support, and outreach at New Cross Hospital, Recovery Near You, and community events.

posts.title: LEAG — Lived Experience Advisory Group Launches Quarterly Meetings
posts.body: SUIT launched the Lived Experience Advisory Group (LEAG), a formal structure ensuring that service users' voices directly shape the services they receive. Quarterly meetings bring together service users, staff, and partner organisations to review feedback, set priorities, and hold services accountable. The 'You said, we did' format ensures transparency and genuine co-production.

posts.title: Karolina's Music Video — Finding Voice Through Song
posts.body: Karolina Sowinska, SUIT's Polish-Speaking Support Worker, features in a powerful music video that captures her journey and the strength she brings to others. Karolina started as a volunteer with SUIT, driven by a desire to support people experiencing hardship, particularly those facing language barriers, difficulties with refugee status, and the challenges of migration. The video is a testament to the creativity and courage of the SUIT family.
posts.quote: Everyone deserves to have supporters on their side, to keep believing in them, to help them achieve. To keep being different, unique, valued. To survive.

art.title: Art & Visual Arts
art.description: Weekly Art Group, exhibitions at Wolverhampton Art Gallery, #poPARTofrecovery, The Forest Faced, and the [P R O] F I L E residency with Asylum Artist Quarter.

music.title: Music & Wellbeing
music.description: Collaborative music-making, songwriting, and rhythm sessions as therapeutic tools for emotional expression and recovery.

drama.title: Drama & Performance
drama.description: Community theatre with Geese Theatre Company, performances at the Arena Theatre, spoken word at the Wolverhampton Literature Festival, and immersive drama at national conferences.
